{"session_id":"9253a465-f575-42a6-b08a-7f38c3fa09bc","transcript_path":"/home/mcrowe/.claude/projects/-home-mcrowe-Programming-AI-isolated-environment-main/9253a465-f575-42a6-b08a-7f38c3fa09bc.jsonl","cwd":"/home/mcrowe/Programming/AI/isolated-environment/main","permission_mode":"bypassPermissions","effort":{"level":"high"},"hook_event_name":"Stop","stop_hook_active":false,"last_assistant_message":"**Arch Linux steps:**

**1. Install deps**
```bash
sudo pacman -Syu --needed curl dialog freerdp git iproute2 libnotify openbsd-netcat
```

**2. Set up Windows VM** — pick one backend:
- **Docker/Podman** (recommended, automated): follow [docs/docker.md](https://github.com/winapps-org/winapps/blob/main/docs/docker.md)
- **libvirt** (more control): follow [docs/libvirt.md](https://github.com/winapps-org/winapps/blob/main/docs/libvirt.md)

Both use KVM under the hood — make sure `kvm` and `kvm_intel`/`kvm_amd` modules load.

**3. Create config**
```bash
mkdir -p ~/.config/winapps
cat > ~/.config/winapps/winapps.conf <<'EOF'
RDP_USER="MyWindowsUser"
RDP_PASS="MyWindowsPassword"
RDP_IP="127.0.0.1"
WAFLAVOR="docker"   # or podman/libvirt
EOF
chmod 600 ~/.config/winapps/winapps.conf
```

**4. Test FreeRDP**
```bash
xfreerdp3 /u:"MyWindowsUser" /p:"MyWindowsPassword" /v:127.0.0.1:3389 /cert:tofu
```
Windows desktop should appear. Disconnect if it works.

**5. Run installer**
```bash
bash <(curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/winapps-org/winapps/main/setup.sh)
```

**Arch gotchas:**
- FreeRDP must be v3+ — `pacman`'s `freerdp` package is v3 now, so you're fine
- `REMOVABLE_MEDIA="/run/media"` is already correct default for Arch (udisks2 mounts there)
- If using Podman, prefix the xfreerdp test with `podman unshare --rootless-netns`
- KVM: add yourself to `kvm` group: `sudo usermod -aG kvm $USER`","background_tasks":[],"session_crons":[]}

stdin_parsed.last_assistant_message:
**Arch Linux steps:**

**1. Install deps**
```bash
sudo pacman -Syu --needed curl dialog freerdp git iproute2 libnotify openbsd-netcat
```

**2. Set up Windows VM** — pick one backend:
- **Docker/Podman** (recommended, automated): follow [docs/docker.md](https://github.com/winapps-org/winapps/blob/main/docs/docker.md)
- **libvirt** (more control): follow [docs/libvirt.md](https://github.com/winapps-org/winapps/blob/main/docs/libvirt.md)

Both use KVM under the hood — make sure `kvm` and `kvm_intel`/`kvm_amd` modules load.

**3. Create config**
```bash
mkdir -p ~/.config/winapps
cat > ~/.config/winapps/winapps.conf <<'EOF'
RDP_USER="MyWindowsUser"
RDP_PASS="MyWindowsPassword"
RDP_IP="127.0.0.1"
WAFLAVOR="docker"   # or podman/libvirt
EOF
chmod 600 ~/.config/winapps/winapps.conf
```

**4. Test FreeRDP**
```bash
xfreerdp3 /u:"MyWindowsUser" /p:"MyWindowsPassword" /v:127.0.0.1:3389 /cert:tofu
```
Windows desktop should appear. Disconnect if it works.

**5. Run installer**
```bash
bash <(curl https://raw.githubusercontent.com/winapps-org/winapps/main/setup.sh)
```

**Arch gotchas:**
- FreeRDP must be v3+ — `pacman`'s `freerdp` package is v3 now, so you're fine
- `REMOVABLE_MEDIA="/run/media"` is already correct default for Arch (udisks2 mounts there)
- If using Podman, prefix the xfreerdp test with `podman unshare --rootless-netns`
- KVM: add yourself to `kvm` group: `sudo usermod -aG kvm $USER`
